title: Splat Configuration
description: JSON Schema for splat N64/PSX/PS2/PSP ROM splitting tool YAML configuration

sha1.description: Expected SHA-1 checksum of the target ROM binary for verification

basename.description: Base name of the project, used for naming the linker script and other generated files

target_path.description: Path to the target binary (ROM file), relative to base_path

base_path.description: Base path of the project; all other paths are relative to this

is_unsupported_platform.description: Disable built-in platform validation checks to allow custom platforms

compiler.description: Compiler used to compile the target binary

endianness.description: Endianness of the target binary (defaults to 'little' for psx/ps2/psp, 'big' for n64)

elf_path.description: Path to the final ELF target, relative to base_path

dump_symbols.description: Dump all symbols to a file for debugging

allow_segment_overrides.description: Allow extension segments to take precedence over built-in segment types

section_order.description: Default section order for generated linker scripts

gp_value.description: Value of the $gp register, used to calculate %gp_rel relocation offsets

o_as_suffix.description: Use .o as the file suffix for all binary files

generated_c_preamble.description: Code inserted at the top of generated C files

generated_s_preamble.description: Code inserted at the top of generated assembly (.s) files

generated_macro_inc_content.description: Extra content appended to the generated macro.inc file

generate_asm_macros_files.description: Regenerate assembly macro include files on each run

include_asm_macro_style.description: Style of the INCLUDE_ASM macro definition

generated_asm_macros_directory.description: Directory for generated assembly macro files, relative to base_path

asset_path.description: Path for extracted asset files, relative to base_path

symbol_addrs_path.description: Path(s) to symbol address files; supports a single path string or a list

build_path.description: Path to the build output directory, relative to base_path

src_path.description: Path to the C/C++ source directory, relative to base_path

asm_path.description: Path to the generated assembly directory, relative to base_path

data_path.description: Path to the extracted data assembly directory, relative to asm_path

nonmatchings_path.description: Path to individual non-matching function assembly files, relative to asm_path

matchings_path.description: Path to matching function assembly files (used with disassemble_all), relative to asm_path

cache_path.description: Path to the splat cache file, relative to base_path

hasm_in_src_path.description: Treat hasm files as relative to src_path instead of asm_path

create_undefined_funcs_auto.description: Generate an undefined_funcs_auto.txt file listing referenced but undefined functions

undefined_funcs_auto_path.description: Path to the generated undefined functions file, relative to base_path

create_undefined_syms_auto.description: Generate an undefined_syms_auto.txt file listing referenced but undefined symbols

undefined_syms_auto_path.description: Path to the generated undefined symbols file, relative to base_path

extensions_path.description: Directory to search for custom splat segment extension Python modules, relative to base_path

lib_path.description: Path to library files linked into the target binary, relative to base_path

o_path.description: Path for object files linked into the target binary, relative to base_path

elf_section_list_path.description: Path to a file listing ELF sections, relative to base_path

subalign.description: Default SUBALIGN value in the generated linker script; null disables it

emit_subalign.description: Emit SUBALIGN directives in the generated linker script

auto_link_sections.description: Sections for which automatic linker script entries are added

ld_script_path.description: Output path for the generated linker script, relative to base_path (defaults to '{basename}.ld')

ld_symbol_header_path.description: Output path for a C header exposing segment RAM/ROM start/end symbols, relative to base_path

ld_discard_section.description: Add a DISCARD section with a wildcard to the linker script

ld_sections_allowlist.description: Sections to preserve during linking (e.g. debug sections)

ld_sections_denylist.description: Sections to discard during linking (does not disable ld_discard_section)

ld_wildcard_sections.description: Use wildcards for section matching in the linker script (e.g. .rodata*)

ld_use_symbolic_vram_addresses.description: Use follows_vram / vram_symbol / follows_classes to compute VRAM addresses symbolically in the linker script

ld_sort_segments_by_vram_class_dependenc.description: Write segments with VRAM class dependencies after the segments they depend on

ld_partial_linking.description: Enable partial linking mode; requires ld_partial_scripts_path and ld_partial_build_segments_path

ld_partial_scripts_path.description: Output directory for per-segment partial linker scripts, relative to base_path

ld_partial_build_segments_path.description: Directory where partially linked segment objects will be placed, relative to base_path

ld_dependencies.description: Generate a .d dependency file alongside each linker script; requires elf_path

ld_dependencies_include.description: Emit -include directives for each object file in the dependency file

ld_legacy_generation.description: Use legacy linker script generation that ignores section_order

segment_end_before_align.description: Place the segment end symbol before the alignment directive

segment_symbols_style.description: Style of auto-generated segment symbols in the linker script

ld_rom_start.description: Starting offset for ROM address symbols in the linker script

ld_fill_value.description: Value for FILL statements in the linker script; null disables FILL statements

ld_bss_is_noload.description: Place BSS sections in NOLOAD segments in the linker script (default: true for n64, false for psx)

ld_align_segment_start.description: Align the start of each segment to this value; null disables

ld_align_segment_vram_end.description: Emit alignment for the *_VRAM_END linker symbol of each segment

ld_align_section_vram_end.description: Emit alignment for the *_END linker symbol of each section within each segment

ld_generate_symbol_per_data_segment.description: Generate a linker symbol for each data file

ld_bss_contains_common.description: Default value for bss_contains_common on all segments

ld_gp_expression.description: Expression for the _gp symbol in the linker script instead of a hardcoded value

create_c_files.description: Create new C stub files for c-type segments when they don't exist

auto_decompile_empty_functions.description: Automatically decompile functions with empty bodies

do_c_func_detection.description: Detect matched/unmatched functions in existing C files to skip generating .s stubs

c_newline.description: Newline character(s) to use in generated C files (compiler-dependent default)

symbol_name_format.description: Format string for default symbol names; use $VRAM, $ROM, $SEG as placeholders

symbol_name_format_no_rom.description: Format string for symbols with no ROM address

find_file_boundaries.description: Detect and report likely file split points during disassembly

pair_rodata_to_text.description: Detect and hint about rodata sections that likely correspond to text sections

migrate_rodata_to_functions.description: Attempt to migrate rodata symbols into their corresponding function .s files

asm_inc_header.description: Header code included at the top of every .s file that is INCLUDE_ASM'd from C (compiler-dependent default)

asm_function_macro.description: Macro used to declare function labels in assembly files

asm_function_alt_macro.description: Macro used for alternate function entry labels in assembly files

asm_jtbl_label_macro.description: Macro used to declare jump table labels in assembly files

asm_data_macro.description: Macro used to declare data symbols in assembly files

asm_end_label.description: Macro used at the end of a function

asm_data_end_label.description: Macro used at the end of a data symbol

asm_ehtable_label_macro.description: Macro used to declare exception handler table labels in assembly files

asm_nonmatching_label_macro.description: Macro used to mark a symbol as non-matching

asm_emit_size_directive.description: Emit .size directives in disassembled files; null uses the compiler default

mnemonic_ljust.description: Number of characters to left-justify mnemonic columns in disassembly output

rom_address_padding.description: Pad ROM addresses with leading zeros in disassembly output

mips_abi_gpr.description: ABI naming convention for general-purpose registers

mips_abi_float_regs.description: ABI naming convention for floating-point registers

named_regs_for_c_funcs.description: Use named registers in disassembly of C function files

add_set_gp_64.description: Add '.set gp=64' directive to asm/hasm files (default: true for n64, false for psx/ps2)

create_asm_dependencies.description: Generate .asmproc.d dependency files for C files that reference assembly

string_encoding.description: Global encoding for rodata strings; can be overridden per segment

data_string_encoding.description: Global encoding for data strings; can be overridden per segment

rodata_string_guesser_level.description: Aggressiveness level for the rodata string guesser (0 = disabled)

data_string_guesser_level.description: Aggressiveness level for the data string guesser (0 = disabled)

allow_data_addends.description: Allow data symbols to use addends on symbol references; can be overridden per symbol

disasm_unknown.description: Attempt to disassemble functions containing unknown instructions instead of falling back to raw data

detect_redundant_function_end.description: Detect and merge redundant/unreferenced function end labels (IDO only)

disassemble_all.description: Disassemble all functions including already-matched ones

make_full_disasm_for_code.description: Emit a full .s file for each c/cpp segment in addition to nonmatchings

global_vram_start.description: Override the detected global VRAM start address (useful for multi-file splat projects)

global_vram_end.description: Override the detected global VRAM end address

use_gp_rel_macro_nonmatching.description: Emit %gp_rel relocation parameters for $gp-relative instructions in nonmatchings

use_gp_rel_macro.description: Emit %gp_rel relocation parameters for $gp-relative instructions in asm/hasm files

suggestion_rodata_section_start.description: Emit suggestions for where the rodata section starts based on data section analysis

header_encoding.description: [N64] Encoding used to decode the ROM header string

gfx_ucode.description: [N64] GFX microcode type used by gfx segments

libultra_symbols.description: [N64] Use named libultra symbols by default

ique_symbols.description: [N64] Use named iQue symbols by default

hardware_regs.description: [N64] Use named hardware register symbols by default

image_type_in_extension.description: [N64] Append the image format type to the output file extension

use_legacy_include_asm.description: [GCC/SN64] Use the legacy INCLUDE_ASM macro format in C files

align_on_branch_labels.description: [PS2/SN] Emit alignment directives on branch labels to work around SN PS2 short loop bug

vram_classes.description: Reusable VRAM address classes for overlay segments that share the same VRAM range

anyOf.description: Long form with optional vram_symbol and follows_classes

name.description: Unique name for this VRAM class

vram.description: Base VRAM address for this class

vram_symbol.description: Linker symbol name to use for the VRAM address of this class

follows_classes.description: List of VRAM class names that this class is placed after

segments.description: List of ROM segments to split

segmentStart.description: ROM offset where this segment starts ('auto' for BSS/SBSS segments without a ROM address)

simpleSegmentType.description: Segment types that take only an optional name argument (no width/height)

imageSegmentType.description: N64 image segment types that require name + width + height

ciSegmentType.description: N64 color-indexed image types that require name + width + height and accept an optional palette name

segmentName.description: Segment name used in output file paths; slashes create subdirectories. Integers are coerced to strings by splat.

segmentEndMarker.description: End marker: [offset] — marks where the previous segment ends

simpleSegmentArray.description: Simple segment: [start, type] or [start, type, name]

imageSegmentArray.description: N64 image segment: [start, type, name, width, height]

ciSegmentArray.description: N64 color-indexed image segment: [start, type, name, width, height] or [start, type, name, width, height, palette_name]

items.description: Palette segment name (defaults to the segment name)

commonSegmentDictProperties.description: Properties common to all dict-form segments

dir.description: Directory prefix for output files (alternative to name for grouping)

vram.description: VRAM base address of this segment

vram_class.description: Name of a vram_class to use for the VRAM address; mutually exclusive with vram_symbol and follows_vram

vram_symbol.description: Linker symbol name used to express this segment's VRAM address; mutually exclusive with vram_class

follows_vram.description: Name of another segment whose VRAM end this segment follows; mutually exclusive with vram_class

align.description: Alignment for this segment in the linker script

subalign.description: SUBALIGN value for this top-level segment (overrides global subalign)

extract.description: Whether to extract/write output files for this segment

exclusive_ram_id.description: Tag used to mark segments that occupy the same VRAM range (overlays)

linker_entry.description: Whether to emit a linker entry for this segment

find_file_boundaries.description: Override the global find_file_boundaries option for this segment

section_order.description: Override the global section order for this segment

symbol_name_format.description: Override the global symbol_name_format for symbols in this segment

symbol_name_format_no_rom.description: Override the global symbol_name_format_no_rom for symbols in this segment

path.description: Explicit file path for this segment's output, overriding default naming

bss_contains_common.description: Whether the BSS section of this segment contains COMMON symbols

linker_section_order.description: Override the linking order of this segment's section in the linker script

linker_section.description: Override the actual section name used when linking this segment

ld_fill_value.description: Override the global ld_fill_value for this segment (top-level only)

ld_align_segment_start.description: Align the start of this segment to the given value, overriding the global setting

suggestion_rodata_section_start.description: Override the global suggestion_rodata_section_start for this segment

pair_segment.description: Name of the segment this segment is paired with for rodata migration

bss_size.description: Size in bytes of the BSS region appended to this code segment's VRAM range

subsegments.description: Child segments within this code segment

topLevelSegment.description: A top-level ROM segment: end marker, typed array, or dict

subsegment.description: A subsegment within a code or group segment: end marker, typed array, or dict

vram.description: Override VRAM address for this subsegment (useful for BSS subsegments)
